fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arizona Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to arizona, speech-language pathologist compact arizona, arizona speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Department of Health Services — Speech-Language Pathology

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Arizona.

license_types.title: Temporary SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. Allows supervised practice in Arizona.

steps.title: Complete Required Education
steps.description: Obtain a master's degree or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation (CAA).

steps.title: Complete a Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete a supervised clinical fellowship of at least 1260 hours within 36 months under the guidance of a licensed speech-language pathologist.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination and Apply
steps.description: Pass the National Examination in Speech-Language Pathology and submit a completed application, official transcripts, clinical fellowship report, and required fees to the Arizona Department of Health Services.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
Arizona DPS fingerprint clearance card required.

telepractice_text: Arizona permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. The compact allows SLPs licensed in member states to practice across state lines through a compact privilege.
provisional_text: Arizona issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship, allowing supervised practice.
reciprocity_text: Arizona is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Arizona through a compact privilege. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available for non-compact applicants.

faqs.question: How much does an Arizona SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $150. Biennial renewal is $100.

faqs.question: Is Arizona part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arizona has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Arizona allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Arizona have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Arizona?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Arizona is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Arizona through a compact privilege. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available for non-compact applicants.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Arizona
hero.dek: Arizona Speech-Language Pathology license requires a master's degree, completion of a clinical fellowship, and passing the national examination.
